conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: For the message queue, I want to use Amazon SQS. It's fully managed and integrates with our AWS stack.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: SQS is reliable. I'll set up the dead-letter queues.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need exactly-once delivery. SQS doesn't guarantee that. Let's use Kafka.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Kafka with idempotent producers can achieve that. I'll set up the brokers.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Kafka has high latency for our real-time processing. Let's use RabbitMQ instead.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: RabbitMQ has low latency and good routing. I'll configure the exchanges.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I just learned about NATS. It has even lower latency than RabbitMQ and is cloud-native. Let's use NATS.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: NATS is newer but promising. I'll set up the JetStream persistence.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We're a small team. We don't need the complexity. SQS is fine. Latency is acceptable. Let's just use SQS.

query: What message queue is the team currently using for asynchronous tasks?
